<article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title1" role="article"><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title1" class="topic nested0" id="resources">
<h1 class="title topictitle1" id="ariaid-title1">Always Free Resources</h1>
<div class="body"><p class="shortdesc">Learn what Always Free resources are available to all Oracle Cloud Infrastructure users.</p>
<p>All Oracle Cloud Infrastructure accounts (whether free or paid) have a set of resources that are free of charge in the <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Identity/regions/managingregions.htm#Home">home region</a> of the tenancy, for the life of the account. These resources display the <strong class="ph uicontrol">Always Free-eligible</strong> label in the Console (for OCI Ampere A1 Compute shapes, see <a class="xref" href="freetier_topic-Always_Free_Resources.htm#compute">Compute</a>).</p>
<p>Using the Always Free resources, you can provision a <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/home.htm">virtual machine (VM) instance</a>, an <a class="xref" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/autonomous-database/dedicated/adbaa/index.html#ADBAA-GUID-B5518C12-0362-4A98-AB35-3CB84AC83F31">Oracle Autonomous Database</a>, and the networking, load balancing, and storage resources needed to support the applications that you want to build. With these resources, you can do things like run small-scale applications or perform proof-of-concept testing. </p>
<p id="resources__alwaysfreeresources">The following sections summarize the Oracle Cloud Always Free-eligible resources that you can provision in your tenancy.</p>
</div>
<article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title2" class="topic nested1" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Infrastructure">
<h2 class="title topictitle2 vl-include-in-page-toc main-content-header" id="ariaid-title2" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="2" aria-label="Infrastructure">Infrastructure</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h2>
<div class="body">
</div>
<article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title3" class="topic nested2" id="untitled1">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title3" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Certificates">Certificates</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get five certificate authorities (CAs) and 150 certificates included in the
      Always Free resources.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title4" class="topic nested2" id="compute">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title4" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Compute">Compute</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get a set of Always Free resources in the Compute service for creating <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/home.htm">compute virtual machine (VM) instances</a>. You must create the Always Free compute instances in your <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/GSG/Reference/faq.htm#How">home region</a>.</p>
<div class="uk-alert uk-alert-primary"><span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="file-edit"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><path fill="none" stroke="#000" d="M18.65,1.68 C18.41,1.45 18.109,1.33 17.81,1.33 C17.499,1.33 17.209,1.45 16.98,1.68 L8.92,9.76 L8,12.33 L10.55,11.41 L18.651,3.34 C19.12,2.87 19.12,2.15 18.65,1.68 L18.65,1.68 L18.65,1.68 Z"></path><polyline fill="none" stroke="#000" points="16.5 8.482 16.5 18.5 3.5 18.5 3.5 1.5 14.211 1.5"></polyline></svg></span><strong> Note</strong><br><br>  If you receive an "out of host capacity" error when trying to create a Compute instance, this indicates a temporary lack of Always Free shapes in your home region. Try creating the instance in a different availability domain, or wait a while, then try to create the instance again. You can also choose to upgrade your account to Pay as You Go or another Paid account type, which gives you access to more types of Compute resources. Remember that Oracle doesn't charge for Always Free resources after you upgrade, and will only charge you for resource usage above the Always Free limits. You can use <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Quotas/Concepts/resourcequotas.htm">compartment quotas</a> to control resource consumption within your account.</div>
<section class="section"><h4 class="title sectiontitle vl-no-in-page-toc">Available Shapes</h4>
<ul>
<li><strong>Micro instances (AMD processor):</strong> All tenancies get up to two Always Free VM instances using the VM.Standard.E2.1.Micro <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/References/computeshapes.htm">shape</a>, which has an AMD processor. </li>
<li><strong>OCI Ampere A1 Compute instances (Arm processor):</strong> All tenancies get the first 3,000 OCPU hours and 18,000 GB hours per month for free for VM instances using the VM.Standard.A1.Flex <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/References/computeshapes.htm">shape</a>, which has an <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/References/arm.htm">Arm processor</a>. For Always Free tenancies, this is equivalent to 4 OCPUs and 24 GB of memory.</li>
</ul>
<p>In regions with multiple availability domains:</p>
<ul>
<li>You can create OCI Ampere A1 Compute instances in any
                        availability domain.</li>
<li>Instances using the VM.Standard.E2.1.Micro shape can only be created in one availability domain.</li>
</ul>
</section>
<section class="section"><h4 class="title sectiontitle vl-no-in-page-toc">Number of Compute Instances Available to Your Account</h4>
<p>Depending on the size of the boot volume and the number of OCPUs that you allocate to each OCI Ampere A1 Compute instance, you can create up to four compute instances. The minimum <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/bootvolumes.htm">boot volume</a> size for each instance is 47 GB, regardless of shape. Your account comes with 200 GB of Always Free
<a class="xref" href="freetier_topic-Always_Free_Resources.htm#blockvolume">block volume</a> storage which you use to create the boot volumes for your compute instances.</p>
<p>For example, using the default boot volume size of 47 GB, you could provision two
                instances using the VM.Standard.E2.1.Micro shape, and two OCI Ampere A1 Compute instances that each have 2 OCPUs. Or,
                you could provision four OCI Ampere A1 Compute instances
                with 1 OCPU each, and zero instances using the VM.Standard.E2.1.Micro shape. Many
                combinations are possible, depending on how you allocate your block storage and OCI Ampere A1 Compute OCPUs. See <a class="xref" href="freetier_topic-Always_Free_Resources.htm#Details_of_the_Always_Free_Compute_instance__a1_flex">Details of the Always Free compute
                    instances</a> for more information on allocating OCPU and memory resources
                when creating OCI Ampere A1 Compute instances.</p>
</section>
<section class="section"><h4 class="title sectiontitle vl-no-in-page-toc">IP Addresses for Compute Instances</h4>
<p> You do not
                have to assign a public IPv4 address to every compute instance in your tenancy. You
                can create a compute instance in a public subnet without assigning the instance a
                public IP addresses, and create an instance in a private subnet.</p>
</section>
<section class="section" id="compute__idleinstances"><h4 class="title sectiontitle vl-no-in-page-toc">Idle Compute Instances</h4>
<div class="uk-alert uk-alert-success"><span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="check"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><polyline fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" points="4,10 8,15 17,4"></polyline></svg></span><strong> Important</strong><br><br>
<p><strong>Reclamation of Idle Compute Instances</strong></p>
<p>Idle Always Free compute instances may be
                    reclaimed by Oracle. Oracle will deem virtual machine and bare metal compute
                    instances as idle if, during a 7-day period, the following are true:</p>
<ul>
<li>CPU utilization for the 95th percentile is less than 20%</li>
<li>Network utilization is less than 20%</li>
<li>Memory utilization is less than 20% <em>(applies to <a class="xref" href="freetier_topic-Always_Free_Resources.htm#Details_of_the_Always_Free_Compute_instance__a1_flex">A1 shapes</a> only)</em></li>
</ul>
</div>
</section>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_Compute_instance"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title5" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-1" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free compute instances</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-1" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title5"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title5" class="topic nested3">
<div class="body">
<p><strong>VM.Standard.E2.1.Micro shape (AMD):</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Processor:</strong> 1/8th of an OCPU with the ability to use additional CPU resources</li>
<li><strong>Memory:</strong> 1 GB</li>
<li><strong>Networking:</strong> Includes one <strong title="Enables an instance to connect to a Virtual Cloud Network (VCN) and determines how the instance connects with endpoints inside and outside the VCN. Each instance automatically comes with a primary VNIC, and you can add secondary ones. Other types of cloud resources, such as load balancers and DB systems, also automatically get a VNIC when they are created." uk-tooltip="" tabindex="0">VNIC <span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="icon: info; ratio: .8"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><path d="M12.13,11.59 C11.97,12.84 10.35,14.12 9.1,14.16 C6.17,14.2 9.89,9.46 8.74,8.37 C9.3,8.16 10.62,7.83 10.62,8.81 C10.62,9.63 10.12,10.55 9.88,11.32 C8.66,15.16 12.13,11.15 12.14,11.18 C12.16,11.21 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 C12.08,11.95 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 L12.13,11.59 Z M11.56,5.67 C11.56,6.67 9.36,7.15 9.36,6.03 C9.36,5 11.56,4.54 11.56,5.67 L11.56,5.67 Z"></path><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" cx="10" cy="10" r="9"></circle></svg></span></strong> with one public IP address and up to 50 Mbps network bandwidth via the internet. Traffic to private IPs, on-premise endpoints via a <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingDRGs.htm">Dynamic Routing Gateway</a>, or to endpoints within the same <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/regions.htm">Oracle Cloud region</a> is up to 480 Mbps.</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Image:</strong> Your choice of one of the following Always Free-eligible <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/References/images.htm">images</a>:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Oracle Linux Cloud Developer 8</p>
<p><a class="xref" href="/iaas/oracle-linux/developer/index.htm">Oracle Linux Cloud Developer</a> provides the latest development tools, languages and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure software development kits (SDKs) to rapidly launch a comprehensive development environment.
Due to the amount of memory allocated to the VM.Standard.E2.1.Micro shape, some programs are not installed.</p>
</li>
<li>Oracle Linux</li>
<li>Ubuntu</li>
<li>CentOS</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p><span id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_Compute_instance__a1_flex"><strong>VM.Standard.A1.Flex shape (Arm-based OCI Ampere A1 Compute):</strong></span> Because the VM.Standard.A1.Flex shape is a <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/References/computeshapes.htm#flexible">flexible shape</a>, you can customize the number of OCPUs and amount of memory that are allocated when you create or resize an instance. You can use all of the Always Free OCPUs and memory to create a single instance, or create multiple smaller instances that each use a portion of the resources.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Processor:</strong>
4 OCPUs total, which you can allocate flexibly</li>
<li><strong>Memory:</strong>
24 GB total, which you can allocate flexibly</li>
<li><strong>Networking:</strong> The network bandwidth and number of VNICs scale proportionately with the number of OCPUs. For details, see <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/References/computeshapes.htm#flexible">Flexible Shapes</a>.</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Image:</strong> Your choice of one of the following Always Free-eligible <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Compute/References/images.htm">images</a>:</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>Oracle Linux Cloud Developer</p>
<p><a class="xref" href="/iaas/oracle-linux/developer/index.htm">Oracle Linux Cloud Developer</a> provides the latest development tools, languages and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure software development kits (SDKs) to rapidly launch a comprehensive development environment.
The Oracle Linux Cloud Developer image requires at least 8 GB of memory.</p>
</li>
<li>Oracle Linux</li>
<li>Ubuntu</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p>For steps to create an Always Free-eligible compute instance, see <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/GSG/Reference/overviewworkflow.htm">Tutorial - Launching Your First Linux Instance</a>.</p>
<div class="uk-alert uk-alert-warning"><span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="lifesaver"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" cx="10" cy="10" r="9"></circle><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" cx="10" cy="10" r="5"></circle><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="5.17" y1="2.39" x2="8.11" y2="5.33"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="5.33" y1="8.11" x2="2.39" y2="5.17"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="14.83" y1="17.61" x2="11.89" y2="14.67"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="14.67" y1="11.89" x2="17.61" y2="14.83"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="17.61" y1="5.17" x2="14.67" y2="8.11"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="11.89" y1="5.33" x2="14.83" y2="2.39"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="8.11" y1="14.67" x2="5.17" y2="17.61"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="2.39" y1="14.83" x2="5.33" y2="11.89"></line></svg></span><strong> Tip</strong><br><br> The Linux images labeled "Always Free Eligible" in the Console are compatible with Always Free compute instances and incur no licensing fees. These images are also compatible with paid resources and are available to users of paid accounts. To provision a compute instance with an image that is not Always Free-eligible, you must have a paid account or a Free Trial account with available credits.</div>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title6" class="topic nested2" id="blockvolume">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title6" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Block Volume">Block Volume</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p id="blockvolume__block">All tenancies receive a total of 200 GB of <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/overview.htm">Block Volume</a> storage, and five volume backups included in the Always Free resources. These amounts apply to both boot volumes and block volumes combined. When you provision a compute instance, the instance automatically receives a 50 GB <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/bootvolumes.htm">boot volume</a> for storage. You can also create and attach block volumes to expand the storage capacity of a compute instance. For more information, see <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/creatingavolume.htm">Creating a Block Volume</a> and <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/attachingavolume.htm">Attaching a Block Volume to an Instance</a>.</p>
<div class="uk-alert uk-alert-success"><span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="check"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><polyline fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" points="4,10 8,15 17,4"></polyline></svg></span><strong> Important</strong><br><br>
<ul>
<li>To create an Always Free block volume, the volume must be created in the <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Identity/regions/managingregions.htm#Home">home region</a> of the tenancy. Volumes created outside of the home region incur regular block volume costs.</li>
<li>For paid or free trial accounts that are eligible for Always Free resources, the same 200 GB of Always Free volume resources and five Always Free volume backup resources are available. When one of your Always Free resources is deleted, some of your Always Free resource capacity becomes available. When you have available Always Free capacity, Block Volume automatically tries to transition an existing paid resource to an Always Free resource. For example, if you have four 50 GB Always Free volumes and one 50 GB paid volume, and you delete one of the Always Free volumes, the service converts the paid volume to an Always Free volume.</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_Block_Volume_resources"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title7" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-2" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free Block Volume resources</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-2" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title7"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title7" class="topic nested3">
<div class="body">
<ul>
<li>200 GB total of combined boot volume and block volume Always Free
Block Volume storage in the <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Identity/regions/managingregions.htm#Home">home region</a>.</li>
<li>Five total volume backups (boot volume and block volume combined) in home
                region.</li>
</ul>
<p>When you create a compute instance, the default <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/bootvolumes.htm">boot volume</a> size for the instance is 50 GB, which counts toward your allotment of 200 GB. You can customize the instance's boot volume size up to 200 GB; however, this will use up your full allotment of storage for Always Free
Block Volume resources. Also, because the minimum boot volume size allowed for compute instances is 50 GB, launching four instances will use all your Always Free
Block Volume resources. Alternatively, you can launch one instance with the default boot volume size of 50 GB, and then create and attach a 150 GB block volume to expand the storage capacity of the instance. For more information, see <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/creatingavolume.htm">Creating a Block Volume</a> and <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/attachingavolume.htm">Attaching a Block Volume to an Instance</a>. Although it is possible to mix paid and Always Free resources, Oracle does not recommend this. If you have used up your allotment of Always Free
Block Volume resources, you can free up block storage resources by deleting an Always Free instance and deleting the boot volume, or deleting an Always Free block volume.</p>
<p>You can have a maximum of five Always Free volume backups at any time. This applies to both boot volume and block volume backups. For example, you could have three boot volume backups for your Always Free instance and two block volume backups for your Always Free block volumes. In this example, if you try to create new backups, the operation will fail with an error until you delete existing Always Free volume backups. For more information about volume backups, see <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/blockvolumebackups.htm">Overview of Block Volume Backups</a> and <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/bootvolumebackups.htm">Overview of Boot Volume Backups</a>.</p>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title8" class="topic nested2" id="objectstorage">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title8" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Object and Archive Storage">Object and Archive Storage</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get a total of 20 GB of Always Free
<a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/objectstorageoverview.htm">Overview of Object Storage</a>.</p>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_Object_Storage_resources"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title9" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-3" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free
        Object Storage resources</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-3" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title9"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title9" class="topic nested3">
<div class="body">
<p id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_Object_Storage_resources__object">If your Free Trial has expired and your account is in an Always Free only state, Always Free includes the following:</p>
<ul>
<li>20 GB of combined <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/understandingstoragetiers.htm#understandingobjectstoragetiers_topic-Standard_Tier">Standard</a> tier, <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/understandingstoragetiers.htm#understandingobjectstoragetiers_topic-Infrequent_Access">Infrequent Access</a> tier, and <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/understandingstoragetiers.htm#understandingobjectstoragetiers_topi-Archive">Archive</a> tier data</li>
<li>50,000 <a class="xref" href="/iaas/api/#/en/objectstorage/latest/">Object Storage API</a> requests per month</li>
</ul>
<p>If you have a paid account or have free credits as part of a Free Trial, Always Free includes the following:</p>
<ul>
<li>10 GB of <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/understandingstoragetiers.htm#understandingobjectstoragetiers_topic-Standard_Tier">Standard</a> tier data </li>
<li>10 GB of <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/understandingstoragetiers.htm#understandingobjectstoragetiers_topic-Infrequent_Access">Infrequent Access</a> tier data</li>
<li>10 GB of <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Object/Concepts/understandingstoragetiers.htm#understandingobjectstoragetiers_topi-Archive">Archive</a> tier data </li>
<li>50,000 <a class="xref" href="/iaas/api/#/en/objectstorage/latest/">Object Storage API</a> requests per month</li>
</ul>
<div class="uk-alert uk-alert-success"><span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="check"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><polyline fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" points="4,10 8,15 17,4"></polyline></svg></span><strong> Important</strong><br><br>  If you are participating in an Oracle Cloud Free Trial, you can store unlimited data and can use 20 GB for free (your usage of the first 20 GB incurs no deduction of your initial $300 trial credit balance). Upgrade to a paid account to continue access to unlimited storage. If you do not upgrade before your trial ends, your free account will be limited to 20 GB of combined <strong>Standard</strong> tier, <strong>Infrequent Access</strong> tier, and <strong>Archive</strong> tier data. If you are using more than the 20-GB limit when your Free Trial ends, all of your objects will be deleted. You can then upload objects until you reach your Always Free usage limits.</div>
<p>See <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/addingbuckets.htm">Putting Data into Object Storage</a> for instructions on using your Always Free storage resources.</p>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title10" class="topic nested2" id="vault">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title10" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Vault">Vault</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All master encryption keys protected by software are free. All tenancies get 20 key
            versions of master encryption keys protected by a hardware security module (HSM) and 150
                Always Free <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/KeyManagement/Concepts/keyoverview.htm">Vault</a> secrets. You can spread these keys or
            secrets across any number of vaults in the tenancy, although virtual private vaults are
            not included in the Always Free resources.</p>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_Vault_resources"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title11" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-4" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free Vault resources</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-4" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title11"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title11" class="topic nested3">
<div class="body">
<ul>
<li>all key versions of a master encryption key protected by software (across any number
                of keys or vaults)</li>
<li>20 total key versions of a master encryption key protected by an HSM (across any
                number of keys or vaults)</li>
<li>
<p>150 total Always Free secrets (across any
                    number of vaults).</p>
</li>
<li>40 secret versions of any given secret (including up to 20 in some form of active
                use and 20 pending deletion).</li>
</ul>
<p>If you have used up your allotment of Always Free
            secrets, you can release resources by scheduling a secret or secret version for
            deletion. At minimum, you do have to wait a day before the secret or secret version is
            deleted.</p>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title12" class="topic nested2" id="resourcemanager">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title12" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Resource Manager">Resource Manager</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get a set of Always Free resources in the <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/ResourceManager/home.htm">Resource Manager</a> service that allow you to automate the process of provisioning infrastructure using Terraform. See <a class="xref" href="freetier_topic-Always_Free_Resources_Launching.htm#freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Launching" title="Learn how to automatically create a full set of Always Free resources in a few minutes using the Resource Manager service's templates feature." uk-tooltip="">Quickly Launch Your Always Free Resources Using Resource Manager</a> for instructions on using Resource Manager to create your Always Free resources.</p>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="resourceref_topic_Details_of_the_Always_Free_Resource_Manager_resources"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title13" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-5" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free

</div></div></div></div></article></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title14" class="topic nested1" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Oracle_Database">
<h2 class="title topictitle2 vl-include-in-page-toc main-content-header" id="ariaid-title14" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="2" aria-label="Database">Database</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h2>
<div class="body">
<p><strong>Oracle Autonomous Database:</strong> All tenancies get two Always Free
<a class="xref" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/autonomous-database/dedicated/adbaa/index.html#ADBAA-GUID-B5518C12-0362-4A98-AB35-3CB84AC83F31">Oracle Autonomous Databases</a>. You can use these databases for transaction processing, data warehousing, Oracle APEX application development, or JSON-based application development. For current regional availability, see the <strong>Always Free Cloud Services</strong> table in <a class="xref" href="https://www.oracle.com/cloud/data-regions.html">Cloud Regions—Infrastructure and Platform Services</a>.</p>
<p><strong>Oracle NoSQL Database:</strong> All tenancies get an Oracle NoSQL Database with up to 133 million reads per month, 133 million writes per month, and 3 tables with 25 GB storage per table. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/nosql-database/index.html">Learn more</a> about Oracle NoSQL Database.</p>
<p><strong>Oracle MySQL HeatWave:</strong> All tenancies get a standalone MySQL HeatWave DB system with a single node HeatWave cluster in the home region. The Always Free DB system has 50 GB of storage to store data and log files. An extra 50 GB of backup storage is available. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/mysql-database/home.htm">Learn more</a> about Oracle MySQL HeatWave.</p>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_Oracle_Autonomous_Database_instance"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title15" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-6" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free Oracle Autonomous Database instance</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-6" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title15"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title15" class="topic nested2">
<div class="body">
<div class="bodydiv">
<ul>
<li><strong>Processor:</strong> 1 Oracle CPU processor  (cannot be scaled)</li>
<li><strong>Database Storage:</strong> 20 GB storage (cannot be scaled)</li>
<li><strong>Workload Type:</strong> You choose from the following workload types: Autonomous Database for Transaction Processing and Mixed Workloads, Autonomous JSON Database, Oracle APEX Application Development, or Autonomous Database for Analytics and Data Warehousing. See <a class="xref" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/autonomous-database/adbsa/about-autonomous-database-workloads.html#GUID-E1C8C5F2-22FB-4225-A3B9-9E78277A5834">About Autonomous Database Workload
                        Types</a> for details about each workload type.</li>
<li><strong>Maximum Simultaneous Database Sessions:</strong> 20</li>
<li><strong>Exadata Infrastructure Type:</strong>
Serverless</li>
</ul>
<div class="uk-alert uk-alert-warning"><span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="lifesaver"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" cx="10" cy="10" r="9"></circle><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" cx="10" cy="10" r="5"></circle><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="5.17" y1="2.39" x2="8.11" y2="5.33"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="5.33" y1="8.11" x2="2.39" y2="5.17"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="14.83" y1="17.61" x2="11.89" y2="14.67"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="14.67" y1="11.89" x2="17.61" y2="14.83"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="17.61" y1="5.17" x2="14.67" y2="8.11"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="11.89" y1="5.33" x2="14.83" y2="2.39"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="8.11" y1="14.67" x2="5.17" y2="17.61"></line><line fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" x1="2.39" y1="14.83" x2="5.33" y2="11.89"></line></svg></span><strong> Tip</strong><br><br> Always Free
Autonomous Databases can be upgraded to paid
                instances after provisioning if you need features like more storage or CPU
                scaling.</div>
<div class="uk-alert uk-alert-primary"><span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="file-edit"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><path fill="none" stroke="#000" d="M18.65,1.68 C18.41,1.45 18.109,1.33 17.81,1.33 C17.499,1.33 17.209,1.45 16.98,1.68 L8.92,9.76 L8,12.33 L10.55,11.41 L18.651,3.34 C19.12,2.87 19.12,2.15 18.65,1.68 L18.65,1.68 L18.65,1.68 Z"></path><polyline fill="none" stroke="#000" points="16.5 8.482 16.5 18.5 3.5 18.5 3.5 1.5 14.211 1.5"></polyline></svg></span><strong> Note</strong><br><br>
<ul>
<li> Before creating an Always Free
Autonomous Database, check your home region
                        for Always Free
Autonomous Database support. See <a class="xref" href="https://www.oracle.com/cloud/data-regions.html">Data Regions for Platform and Infrastructure
                            Services</a>. </li>
<li>You cannot create an Always Free
Autonomous Database in a home region where Always Free
Autonomous Databases are not supported. </li>
<li>Not all regions support the same database version. The supported version may
                        be 19c-only or 21c-only, depending on the region.</li></ul>
</div>
<p>See <a class="xref" href="/iaas/autonomous-database-shared/doc/autonomous-always-free.html">Always Free Autonomous Database</a> for
                additional product details.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title16" class="topic nested1" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Networking">
<h2 class="title topictitle2 vl-include-in-page-toc main-content-header" id="ariaid-title16" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="2" aria-label="Networking">Networking</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h2>
<div class="body"></div>
<article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title17" class="topic nested2" id="resourceref_topic-Details_of_the_Always_Free_Cluster_Placement_Groups_resources">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title17" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Cluster Placement Groups">Cluster Placement Groups</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get from 10 to 50 Always Free cluster placement groups in a region, depending on the service's pricing model.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title18" class="topic nested2" id="loadbalancing">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title18" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Load Balancing">Load Balancing</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All Oracle Cloud Infrastructure tenancies
            created December 15, 2020 or later get one Always Free
            Flexible Load Balancer with a minimum and maximum bandwidth set to 10 Mbps.</p>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_load_balancer"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title19" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-7" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free Load Balancer</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-7" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title19"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title19" class="topic nested3">
<div class="body">
<p><strong>Tenancies created on December 15, 2020 or later</strong></p>
<ul id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_load_balancer__ul_ufz_c35_1pb">
<li><strong>Shape:</strong> Flexible (minimum and maximum: 10 Mbps)</li>
<li><strong>Listeners:</strong> 16</li>
<li><strong>Virtual Hostnames:</strong> 16</li>
<li><strong>Backend Sets:</strong> 16</li>
<li><strong>Backend Servers:</strong> 1024</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Tenancies created before December 15, 2020</strong></p>
<ul>
<li id="Details_of_the_Always_Free_load_balancer__loadbalancer"><strong>Shape:</strong> Micro (10 Mbps)</li>
<li><strong>Listeners:</strong> 10</li>
<li><strong>Virtual Hostnames:</strong> 10</li>
<li><strong>Backend Sets:</strong> 10</li>
<li><strong>Backend Servers:</strong> 128</li>
</ul>
<p>For information about provisioning an Always Free load balancer, see <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/loadbalancing.htm">Getting Started with Load Balancing</a>.</p>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title20" class="topic nested2" id="NetworkLoadBalancer">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title20" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Network Load Balancer">Network Load Balancer</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>As part of your Always Free resources, you get one Network Load Balancer.</p>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="NetworkLoadBalancerDetails"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title21" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-8" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free Network Load Balancer</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-8" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title21"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title21" class="topic nested3">
<div class="body">
<ul id="NetworkLoadBalancerDetails__ul_vhx_n35_1pb">
<li>
<p><strong>Listeners:</strong> 50</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Backend sets:</strong> 50</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Backend servers per set:</strong> 512</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Backend servers total:</strong> 1024 (to be distributed among your backend
                    sets)</p>
</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title22" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_VCNs">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title22" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Virtual Cloud Networks (VCNs)">Virtual Cloud Networks (VCNs)</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>Free Tier tenancies (tenancies that are not paid and do not have Free Trial credits) can
            have up to 2 virtual cloud networks (VCNs). A VCN is a software-defined network that you
            set up in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure data centers in a particular
            region. VCNs include IPv4 and IPv6 support.</p>
<p>Tenancies are by default not allowed to send e-mail through outbound TCP port 25 to the internet. If you require the ability to send email from your tenancy, <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/servicelimits.htm#Requesti">open a service limits request</a> to obtain an exemption. </p>
<p>See the following topics for details on Networking service resources and service
            limits:</p>
<ul>
<li><a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/landing.htm">Networking</a>
<em>(service overview)</em></li>
<li><a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingVCNs_topic-Overview_of_VCNs_and_Subnets.htm">Overview of VCNs and Subnets</a></li>
<li><a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/servicelimits.htm#network_limits">Networking Limits</a></li>
</ul>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title23" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_VCN_flow_logs">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title23" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="VCN Flow Logs">VCN Flow Logs</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>To help you audit the traffic in and out of the virtual network interface cards (VNICs) in your VCN, and to troubleshoot your <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/securitylists.htm">security lists</a>, you can set up <em>VCN flow logs</em>. Flow logs record details about traffic that has been accepted or rejected based on the security list rules. Free Tier tenancies (tenancies that are not paid and do not have Free Trial credits) receive up to 10GB per month shared across OCI
<a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/loggingoverview.htm">Logging service</a>.</p>
<p>See <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/vcn-flow-logs.htm">VCN Flow Logs</a> for more information on this feature.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title24" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_VPN_Connect">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title24" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Site-to-Site VPN">Site-to-Site VPN</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>Site-to-Site VPN provides a site-to-site IPSec connection between your on-premises network and your virtual cloud network (VCN). Use up to 50 IPSec connections with your Free Tier account. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Network/Tasks/managingIPsec.htm">Learn more</a>.</p>
</div>
</article></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title25" class="topic nested1" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Management">
<h2 class="title topictitle2 vl-include-in-page-toc main-content-header" id="ariaid-title25" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="2" aria-label="Observability and Management">Observability and Management</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h2>
<article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title26" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Application_Development_Monitoring">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title26" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Application Performance Monitoring">Application Performance Monitoring</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get 1000 Application Performance Monitoring
            tracing events and 10 Synthetic Monitor runs per hour included in the Always Free resources. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/application-performance-monitoring/index.html">Learn more</a>.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title27" class="topic nested2" id="serviceconnectorhub">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title27" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Connector Hub">Connector Hub</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get 2 Always Free
<strong title="The definition of the data to be moved. A connector specifies a source service, target service, and optional tasks." uk-tooltip="" tabindex="0">connectors <span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="icon: info; ratio: .8"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><path d="M12.13,11.59 C11.97,12.84 10.35,14.12 9.1,14.16 C6.17,14.2 9.89,9.46 8.74,8.37 C9.3,8.16 10.62,7.83 10.62,8.81 C10.62,9.63 10.12,10.55 9.88,11.32 C8.66,15.16 12.13,11.15 12.14,11.18 C12.16,11.21 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 C12.08,11.95 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 L12.13,11.59 Z M11.56,5.67 C11.56,6.67 9.36,7.15 9.36,6.03 C9.36,5 11.56,4.54 11.56,5.67 L11.56,5.67 Z"></path><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" cx="10" cy="10" r="9"></circle></svg></span></strong>. Connector Hub helps cloud engineers manage and move data between Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) services and from OCI to third-party services. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/connector-hub/home.htm">Learn more</a>.</p>
</div>
<div class="uk-margin-top uk-accordion" uk-accordion=""><div id="sch-details"><a class="uk-accordion-title" href="#" id="ariaid-title28" role="button" aria-controls="uk-accordion-9" aria-expanded="false" aria-disabled="false">Details of the Always Free
        Connector Hub resources</a><div class="uk-accordion-content" hidden="" id="uk-accordion-9" role="region" aria-labelledby="ariaid-title28"><div aria-labelledby="ariaid-title28" class="topic nested3">
<div class="body">
<p id="sch-details__object">If you have a free account (including trial accounts), Always Free
Connector Hub includes 2 Always Free
<strong title="The definition of the data to be moved. A connector specifies a source service, target service, and optional tasks." uk-tooltip="" tabindex="0">connectors <span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="icon: info; ratio: .8"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><path d="M12.13,11.59 C11.97,12.84 10.35,14.12 9.1,14.16 C6.17,14.2 9.89,9.46 8.74,8.37 C9.3,8.16 10.62,7.83 10.62,8.81 C10.62,9.63 10.12,10.55 9.88,11.32 C8.66,15.16 12.13,11.15 12.14,11.18 C12.16,11.21 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 C12.08,11.95 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 L12.13,11.59 Z M11.56,5.67 C11.56,6.67 9.36,7.15 9.36,6.03 C9.36,5 11.56,4.54 11.56,5.67 L11.56,5.67 Z"></path><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" cx="10" cy="10" r="9"></circle></svg></span></strong>.</p>
<p>If you have a paid account, see <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/servicelimits.htm#serviceconnectorhub-limits">Connector Hub Limits</a>.</p>
</div>
</div></div></div></div></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title29" class="topic nested2" id="dashboards">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title29" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Console Dashboards">Console Dashboards</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get 100 dashboards per tenancy included in the Always Free resources. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Dashboards/Concepts/dashboardsoverview.htm">Learn more</a>.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title30" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Notifications">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title30" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Email Delivery">Email Delivery</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>As part of your Always Free resources, you can send 3000 emails for free per month. Learn more about OCI's <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Email/home.htm">Email Delivery service</a>.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title31" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_always_Free_Resources_Fleet_management">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title31" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Fleet Application Management">Fleet Application Management</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies are entitled to manage the system lifecycle operation of the first 25 resources per month, which include Compute instances and databases, for free as a part of their Always Free resources.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title32" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Monitoring">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title32" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Monitoring">Monitoring</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>All tenancies get 500 million <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Monitoring/home.htm">Monitoring service</a> ingestion data points, and 1 billion retrieval data points included in the Always Free resources.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title33" class="topic nested2" id="unique_1003083812">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title33" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Notifications">Notifications</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>As part of your Always Free resources, you can send 1 million https notifications per month, and 1000 email notifications per month. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Notification/home.htm">Learn more</a> about OCI's Notifications service.</p>
</div>
</article></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title34" class="topic nested1" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Additional_Services">
<h2 class="title topictitle2 vl-include-in-page-toc main-content-header" id="ariaid-title34" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="2" aria-label="Additional Services">Additional Services</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h2>
<div class="body">
</div>
<article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title35" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Outbound_Data_Transfer">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title35" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Outbound Data Transfer">Outbound Data Transfer</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>As part of your Always Free resources, you get 10 TB per
            month of outbound data.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title36" class="topic nested2" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Logging">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title36" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Logging">Logging</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>Logging provides a highly scalable and fully managed single pane of glass for all the logs in your tenancy. <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Logging/Concepts/loggingoverview.htm">Learn more</a> about the Logging service.</p>
</div>
</article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title37" class="topic nested2" id="resourceref_topic_Bastions">
<h3 class="title topictitle3 main-content-header" id="ariaid-title37" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="3" aria-label="Bastion">Bastion</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h3>
<div class="body">
<p>OCI's Bastion service provides restricted and time-limited Secure Shell Protocol (SSH) access to target resources that don't have public endpoints. Bastion is free for both free and paid accounts. See <a class="xref" href="/iaas/Content/Bastion/Concepts/bastionoverview.htm">Bastion</a> for more information.</p>
</div>
</article></article><article aria-labelledby="ariaid-title38" class="topic nested1" id="freetier_topic_Always_Free_Resources_Service_Limits">
<h2 class="title topictitle2 vl-include-in-page-toc main-content-header" id="ariaid-title38" tabindex="-1" role="presentation"><span role="heading" aria-level="2" aria-label="Service Usage and Limits">Service Usage and Limits</span><button type="button" class="copy_button" aria-label="Copy link to this section" title="Copy link to this section" tabindex="0">🔗</button></h2>
<div class="body">
<p>You can find your tenancy's limits for Always Free resources in the Console. To check these limits: Open the <strong title="Main menu located in the upper-left corner of the Oracle Cloud Console. Use the menu to navigate to OCI services, dashboards, and marketplace." uk-tooltip="" tabindex="0">navigation menu <span class="vl-1px-up uk-icon" uk-icon="icon: info; ratio: .8"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20"><path d="M12.13,11.59 C11.97,12.84 10.35,14.12 9.1,14.16 C6.17,14.2 9.89,9.46 8.74,8.37 C9.3,8.16 10.62,7.83 10.62,8.81 C10.62,9.63 10.12,10.55 9.88,11.32 C8.66,15.16 12.13,11.15 12.14,11.18 C12.16,11.21 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 C12.08,11.95 12.16,11.35 12.13,11.59 L12.13,11.59 Z M11.56,5.67 C11.56,6.67 9.36,7.15 9.36,6.03 C9.36,5 11.56,4.54 11.56,5.67 L11.56,5.67 Z"></path><circle fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1.1" cx="10" cy="10" r="9"></circle></svg></span></strong> and select <strong class="ph uicontrol">Governance & Administration</strong>. Under <strong class="ph uicontrol">Tenancy Management</strong>, select <strong class="ph uicontrol">Limits, Quotas and Usage</strong>.</p>
</div>
</article></article></article>
